references.title: Modelling student online behaviour in a virtual learning environment

extracted.Features: [ Click Counts Aggregated per Week, Clicks Aggregated per week and Content Type, Bioary indicators for activity in VLE and Content Types]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: LMS logs were aggregated and later the click counts were binned using equal-width to reduce the number of hypothesis created.
extracted.Models:
Heneral Unary Hypotheses Automaton (GUHA),
Markov Chains
extracted.Cluster Description:
Analyzes of students who have had 0 activity in the LMS in at least one of the 4 weeks.

Students are more likely to be at risk if they make a submission at least 2  weeks.
extracted.Implications: Eaarly disconnection followed by an increase in activity is an indicator opf success whereas disconnected behaviour is more likely to be unsuccessful.
extracted.Comments:
Authors make a distinct analysis between students who succeeded in first assignment and students who have not. 

Analysis shows
extracted.Limitations: Undisclosed sample size. Analysis of GUHA is limited to a specific subset of students (those with at least onne week with 0 activity.

annotations.text: The Open University has invested in early warning systems that flag and allow students at risk of failing the module. The paper goes over methods to analyze students in a VLE (Markov Chains and GUHA). While both are effective, Markov Chain analysis allows for the modelling of temporal dependencies.
